text: With the patient in a lateral decubitus position for a conventional anterolateral thoracotomy, a four- or five-centimeter incision is performed with no intercostal retractor and only eventually a retractor for the self-test.
First step is the access and dissection of the posterior carina of the right upper bronchus and the intermediate bronchus.
This step is very important to facilitate the complete dissection in the anterior plane.
After the first step, with a gentle traction, we get to the anterior part of the pulmonary ilium.
First, we dissect the upper pulmonary vein up to get to the anterior trunk, our first objective.
In our hospital, we combine the use of electrocatory dissection and ligature indistantly as shown.
When performing a conscious hilar dissection, it is important not only to properly identify all the vascular structures, but also to start the lymphadenectomy at this level.
Dissection is performed with a satisfactory control of the vascular structures in every moment, even with the presence of a small adventitiole hematoma.

In most of the cases we have previously performed, after dissection and section of the anterior trunk seems easier to dissect and resect the right upper bronchus, especially taking into account the previous dissection of the posterior plane.
After the bronchial resection, the new mobility of the right upper lobe makes visible the dissection of the upper pulmonary vein and the dorsal and caesareal arteries in entirety.

As you can see in this video, conventional instruments can be used for making a conscious and secure dissection of the vascular structures as well as the specific ones designed for endoscopic surgery.
